methodsAttempted.query: site:eur.nl Erasmus University Rotterdam generative AI policy students assessment
methodsAttempted.notes: Found the public Generative AI Usage Guidelines, a teaching guidance page, AI@EUR pages, and library student resources on the official eur.nl domain.
Checked additional official pages for academic integrity, student guidance, employee-only MyEUR links, and a PhD trajectory PDF.

methodsAttempted.query: Policy on the use of GenAI and the PhD trajectory EUR
methodsAttempted.notes: The central guidelines link to an official PDF policy for PhD trajectories.

artifacts.summary: Official EUR discovery located central GenAI usage guidelines, teaching and assessment guidance, an AI@EUR responsible-use page, and a PhD trajectory GenAI PDF. Library and Learn with AI pages were retained only as discovered official resources because they mostly route to modules or training. MyEUR employee pages redirected to SURFconext login and were marked inaccessible.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Policy on the use of GenAI and the PhD-trajectory

artifacts.claimText: EUR's central GenAI guidelines tell staff, faculty, and students to use GenAI as a supplementary tool, remain responsible for delivered work, and be transparent when GenAI has been used.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: EUR's central GenAI guidelines say users should not share personal or confidential information with GenAI tools and may use publicly available or published university information if use also follows the Acceptable Use Policy.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: EUR's central GenAI guidelines state that students must create and submit their own work, that GenAI-generated work submitted as one's own is fraud, and that GenAI use in exams or evaluations where it is not permitted is fraud.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: EUR's teacher-facing GenAI guidance says instructors need to communicate whether and how GenAI is allowed for a course or assignment, and it frames unpermitted student GenAI use as comparable to ghostwriting.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: EUR's AI@EUR page states that the university engages with AI responsibly through Responsible AI by Design, complies with EU AI Act rules, and routes staff and students to MyEUR pages for AI Act and AI-system action guidance.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: EUR's PhD trajectory GenAI policy allows PhD research use of GenAI systems when its principles are followed, prefers licensed or EUR language-model systems, and advises consulting a privacy officer before using another program.

citation.citationTitle: Policy on the use of GenAI and the PhD-trajectory
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Use GenAI as a supplement and not as a replacement. Stay critical. Always check and edit the text before sharing it with others. Ultimately, you remain responsible for the work you deliver and share. If you have used GenAI somewhere, be transparent about it.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: The golden rules for using GenAI; GenAI in education
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Do not mention personal information about yourself, others or the EUR in a question to GenAI. You should not share personal or confidential information with GenAI tools. Data that is publicly available or defined as published university information can be freely used in GenAI tools.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: GenAI does not care about Privacy
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The basic principle is that you always create and submit your own work. You bear full responsibility for your work. If it is not your own work, or generated by a GenAI tool, then that is fraud. Any use of GenAI during exams on-campus or other evaluations where it is indicated that the use of Generative AI is not permitted is fraud and is not permitted.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Generative AI is not allowed, but the student still makes use of these tools. You can compare this to ghostwriting. You will need to communicate to students if and what use of generative AI is allowed for your course/assignment.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Misuse of generative AI; Communicating an AI-course policy
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: At EUR, we are committed to engaging with AI responsibly. We follow the principles of Responsible AI by Design. Erasmus University Rotterdam complies with these regulations and applies additional guidelines grounded in Erasmus values. A key requirement of the AI Act is that everyone in the organisation has sufficient knowledge of AI.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: EUR and AI; How do you meet the requirements of the AI Act?

artifacts.sourceTitle: Policy on the use of GenAI and the PhD-trajectory
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: If the other principles are adhered to it is allowed to use (Gen)AI systems in the PhD-trajectory for research purposes or as a support tool. It is highly recommended that either the licensed GenAI system or the own EUR language model is used. If another program is used it is advised to consult with the privacy officer.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: General principles of legitimate and responsible use of AI for PhD research

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow central GenAI usage-guidance claim with direct original-language evidence.
Approved because the source directly addresses personal and confidential information in GenAI tools.
Approved because the central page explicitly states own-work responsibility and unpermitted GenAI exam/evaluation use as fraud.
Approved as teacher-facing guidance, not as a university-wide course-rule claim.
Approved as a source-status and governance-routing claim, with caveat that linked MyEUR details were inaccessible.
Approved as scoped to the PhD trajectory PDF only.

artifacts.title: Erasmus University Rotterdam AI Policy Staged Findings
artifacts.summary: Staged findings for Erasmus University Rotterdam rely on official EUR pages and one official PDF. The strongest public evidence is the central GenAI Usage Guidelines, which cover responsible use, privacy/confidentiality, transparency, and education-integrity boundaries. Additional official sources support teacher-facing assessment guidance, Responsible AI and AI Act routing, and PhD-trajectory research guidance.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
MyEUR employee pages linked from AI@EUR required SURFconext login and were not used as policy evidence.
Teacher-facing guidance is scoped to communicating course and assignment AI expectations, not to a single fixed university-wide permission rule for every course.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
